title: Blend orthogonal experts with rank-based OOF ensembling
summary: ROC AUC rewards ranking, not calibrated probability scale.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ranking, not calibrated probability scale. That makes direct probability averaging fragile when experts have different score distributions, while rank-based OOF blending is usually safer; calibration is only useful when a probability-level stacker needs comparable scales.
implementation_hint: Train 3 orthogonal experts: current engineered-feature tree model, a raw-categorical CatBoost specialist, and one new horizon/set/sequence specialist. Convert OOF predictions to within-fold ranks or normalized logits, learn a tiny blender on OOF outputs, and use beta calibration only if you stack on raw probabilities rather than ranks.
expected_effect: Often yields a cleaner public-score gain than adding one more heuristic to a single model.
risk: Little benefit if experts are too correlated; fold misalignment can silently leak and overstate CV.
